dialogueChunks.intro: You look like someone who's seen the world, {player_name}. Tell me: do you believe a person can outrun their past?
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Years ago, I abandoned a settlement during a {enemy_type} raid. Just ran. Left everyone behind: friends, neighbors, people who counted on me. I've lived with that shame every day since. Now I've heard that {location} is facing the same threat, and {target_npc} is there, one of the people I left behind. They don't know I'm nearby. I want to make it right: send them {quest_item} to help with their defenses, anonymously, gathered from {location_hint}. I can't face them yet. But I can stop being useless.
dialogueChunks.outro: I know this doesn't erase what I did. But doing nothing a second time would be worse than the first.

playerResponses.text: Trying to make it right takes courage too. I'll help.
Anonymous help? Seems like you're still running. But I'll gather the supplies.
Your guilt isn't my burden. Deal with it yourself.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I've spent a long time on the road trying to forget something I did. The road hasn't helped.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: When I worked at the tavern in {location}, I watered down the medicine supply during a plague to stretch it further and pocket the difference. People died, {player_name}. Not many, maybe two or three, but that's two or three who might have lived if I hadn't been greedy. {target_npc} was the healer who couldn't understand why the remedies weren't working. I need to make amends. There's a source of pure {quest_item} {location_hint} that could restock the settlement's medicine cache properly. Real supplies, this time. No shortcuts.
dialogueChunks.outro: I can't bring back the dead. But I can make sure no one else dies from my selfishness.

playerResponses.text: What you did was terrible. But wanting to fix it is real. I'll help.
People died because of your greed. Getting supplies doesn't even the score.
Turn yourself in. That's the only real redemption.

dialogueChunks.intro: Sit with me for a moment, {player_name}. I have a story I've never told anyone, and it's killing me to keep it inside.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I used to be a guard in {location}. One night during a {enemy_type} attack, I saw a family trapped in a burning building. I had the chance to help them, but the fire was so hot, so close. I froze. By the time I moved, it was too late for the youngest child. {target_npc} was the father. He doesn't know I was there: doesn't know I could have saved his daughter and didn't. He still greets me kindly whenever our paths cross, and every smile cuts deeper than any blade. I need to do something: anything. There's a memorial site {location_hint} that was destroyed by {enemy_type}. If I rebuild it with proper {quest_item}, maybe... maybe I can start to live with myself.
dialogueChunks.outro: I don't expect forgiveness. I just can't carry this alone anymore.

playerResponses.text: Rebuilding won't undo the past, but it honors the memory. I'll help.
That's a heavy burden. I'll gather what you need.
You should tell {target_npc} the truth. Not build monuments to avoid it.
